D8 AnthropologyL4 AnalyzeReference

Sapir-Whorf: Russian Blues — Goluboy vs Siniy

Russian has two basic words for blue (siniy = dark, goluboy = light); Winawer 2007 showed Russian speakers discriminate cross-category pairs faster than within-category — language tweaks perception

Winawer et al. 2007 ran a colour discrimination task: pick which of two blue squares matches a target. When the pair straddled the siniy/goluboy boundary, Russian speakers were ~10% faster than English speakers (who lump both as "blue"). Within-category pairs showed no advantage. Modest but real linguistic relativity.

Drag hue around the siniy/goluboy boundary; the colour bin name follows.
